questions.correctText: George Orwell is the author of the renowned dystopian novel "1984".

questions.q: Which book is widely considered the best-selling single non-religious book of all time?
questions.correctText: "Don Quixote" by Miguel de Cervantes is widely recognized as the best-selling non-religious book of all time, with an estimated 500 million copies sold.

questions.q: In which major city is Charles Dickens' novel "Great Expectations" primarily set?
questions.correctText: The novel "Great Expectations" by Charles Dickens is primarily set in Kent and London during the early to mid-19th century.

questions.q: Which author created the iconic detective character Sherlock Holmes?
questions.correctText: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle is the Scottish writer best known for creating the character Sherlock Holmes.

questions.q: What is the original British title of the first book in the Harry Potter series?
options: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
questions.correctText: The first novel in the Harry Potter series, published in the United Kingdom, is titled "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone".
